title: Modbus Serial Gateway Exposing Registers to Wrong TCP Subnet

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CR 4.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 CR 4.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.40: Modbus Serial Gateway Exposing Registers to Wrong TCP Subnet.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.40' running on edge_hub_modbus, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" transport="tcp" earliest=-24h
| lookup serial_gateway_allowlist.csv dest_ip src_subnet OUTPUT permitted
| where permitted!="true"
| stats dc(function_code) as fcs, sum(register_count) as regs by src_ip, dest_ip
| sort - regs
description: Flags TCP-side Modbus requests from subnets that should not see serial-segment registers, supporting confidentiality on component channels.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes edge_hub_modbus. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) ACL on gateway Ethernet port; (2) VPN overlay for remote; (3) alert new src subnets; (4) document data diodes where used. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge_hub_modbus`; Lookups: `serial_gateway_allowlist`, `serial_gateway_allowlist.csv`.
visualization: Table, Heatmap, Time chart. Key fields: `dest_ip`, `fcs`, `regs`, `src_ip`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Edge Hub Modbus TCP capture.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_modbus`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) ACL on gateway Ethernet port; (2) VPN overlay for remote; (3) alert new src subnets; (4) document data diodes where used.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" transport="tcp" earliest=-24h
| lookup serial_gateway_allowlist.csv dest_ip src_subnet OUTPUT permitted
| where permitted!="true"
| stats dc(function_code) as fcs, sum(register_count) as regs by src_ip, dest_ip
| sort - regs
```

Understanding this SPL

**Modbus Serial Gateway Exposing Registers to Wrong TCP Subnet (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 4.1)** — Flags TCP-side Modbus requests from subnets that should not see serial-segment registers, supporting confidentiality on component channels.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_modbus`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Edge Hub Modbus TCP capture. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_modbus. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where permitted!="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, dest_ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Heatmap, Time chart.
grandmaExplanation: We flags TCP-side Modbus requests from subnets that should not see serial-segment registers, supporting confidentiality on component channels. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
